SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for cloud network security and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: cloud_network_security

resources:
- name: projects/myprojectid/instances/myserverinstance1  # replace this value to your instance id and project ID in GCE (Google Cloud Engine) platform, e.g., myserverinstance1 or 'default' for default Google Compute Engine configuration with a single machine running the latest Linux kernel

   zone: us-central2a    # use appropriate region if not US Central 2A as per your project setup in GCE (Google Cloud Engine) platform, e.g., europe-west1 or 'default' for default Google Compute Engine configuration with a single machine running the latest Linux kernel
   name: myserverinstance1     # replace this value to instance id and type of instances you are using on your project setup in GCE (Google Cloud Engine) platform, e.g., us-central2a or 'default' for default Google Compute Engine configuration with a single machine running the latest Linux kernel
   networkInterfaces:  # replace this value to instance details and type of instances you are using on your project setup in GCE (Google Cloud Engine) platform, e.g., us-central2a or 'default' for default Google Compute Engine configuration with a single machine running the latest Linux kernel
     networkIPv4: 0.0.0.0/0    # use appropriate value if not all IPs are allowed in your project setup on GCE (Google Cloud Engine) platform, e.g., 'all' or specific ip address range for instance details and type of instances you are using
